title: onopordum dissectum subsp. dissectum
intro: Onopordum dissectum subsp. dissectum, also known as the cardoon, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant's leaves and stalks contain a high concentration of inulin, a type of soluble dietary fiber with potential medicinal properties. It has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including constipation and diarrhea, and is believed to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The cardoon can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or dried extracts, and is often used as a natural remedy to support gut health and immune function.
